meaning: n.[希神]塔利亚(主管喜剧和田园诗的女神)
examples: Graces Greek mythology Roman mythology Three sister goddesses, known in Greek mythology as Aglaia, Euphrosyne, and Thalia, who dispense charm and beauty.
The net result is a fairer story—though when books try to be fair there's always a risk of being bland, says Thalia Dragona, a Greek educational psychologist.
When she was allowed to go out without supervision, she went to see old movies at the Thalia and the New Yorker.
Thalia Vasquez was one of hundreds of out-of-town voters who went to a special polling place to vote and had to wait for hours.
Well-known Latin stars like Marc Anthony, Carlos Vives, Thalia and Shakira were largely ignored in favour of new artists, but Spanish crooner Alejandro Sanz also received four nominations.
